old_claim.note: Transoceanic News Agencies provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No transoceanic-agency dependency remains because domestic British telegraphic newsrooms operated in the 1840s before the repository's 1866 global agency node.

source_shape.source_locator: The museum places the Electric Telegraph Company's domestic newsrooms in the telegraph's British expansion from the 1840s.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: Early company newsrooms fed information from London across Britain through the growing national wire network.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Later global agency and cable scale expands the service but cannot be an enabling predecessor of the earlier domestic practice.

ontology_before: Domestic 1840s newsrooms depended on a 1866 transoceanic news-agency system.
ontology_after: The early newsroom service requires telegraphy; transoceanic agencies remain a later scaling path.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: telegraph_news_tickers no longer directly depends on transoceanic_news_agencies.
would_reject_if: The node were narrowed to post-1866 global cable news feeds rather than early British telegraphic newsrooms.
short_reason: The later global agency system cannot enable the earlier service.
